review_text: Exceptional value for money. We booked a sea view villa. It had a modern style and was situated directly opposite the ocean with unobstructed views. Bed was comfortable. Plenty of space. Had everything we needed. The only thing it needs is a good deep clean (high & low dusting, fridge, fan, etc) but it felt clean enough for us.
Awsome place albany

Stayed for a week.  Location is the best things about this park.   Sadly I swept the games room 3 times left the sweepings in the corner as there was no dustpan and it was still there when I left.|Heard the park is changing hands hopefully that comes with better attention to detail but that may come with a higher price.

After being on the road for over one year, we’ve seen a lot of caravan parks all across Oz and I can honestly say this place has the worst showers of all. It wouldn’t hurt to upgrade that building guys… Absolutely not worth the coin if you ask me.

We are a couple and were in a 2 bedrom sea view cabin (#7).
View was terrific, straight across Frenchman's Bay.
Queen bed was very comfortable. 2nd room was Double bed and there was a bunk bed in the lounge/dining room.
Only 3 dinner plates, 4 bowls, 4 mugs for possibly 5 persons.
Good size TV. No internet, normal channels only.
The TV remote was missing batteries cover. The cover we found under the TV cabinet didn't fit. Loose batteries could be an issue with small children.
All spaces were clean and tidy.
Shower nice, hot as you may like.
RC air-conditioning was great as it was quite cold during night, morning and our last afternoon.
Full size fridge freezer.
The freezer requires a serious defrost as the ice build up has pushed the back panel about 10cm towards the door.
BBQ on the verandah probably should be replaced due to rust.

Great place to stay.

BUT! THE ANTS JUST TOOK OVER OUR VAN.

I noticed ant holes all round  when we parked.
so heads up if you stay! Make sure you Check! and put down an Ant Barrier.

We stayed on a van site . It was easy to get into . We saw others that looked a bit difficult.  We were very happy with our spot and the amenities.  The location is lovely  , a stroll to the beach and a  swimming area / pool in the bay . Staff were welcoming.

1 star because the amenities are revolting. Sewer smell, soap scum on shower tiles and toilet cubicles that look like they have shit on them, but it doesn't. Staff are aware but not interested and said only 1hr for a  cleaner per amenities block.
However the campground is great with large sites.

Stayed here for 1 night, last minute booking whilst travelling.
Caravan park was quiet, with friendly staff.
Large spacious sites with grass.
Nice camp kitchen available, that has basic amenities.
Nice play ground for the kids
Park is outdated although has all the comforts required.
Outstanding! The VIEW! Affordable, clean, comfortable, peaceful Seaview Villa. Booked next year trip today. Highly recommended.

Stayed overnight on powered tent site while other family members stayed on powered caravan site near us. Was a bit nervous after reading reviews but we had no issues with our short stay. Staff were all lovely and friendly, office ladies and grounds staff. Yes it is old and tired but we found our sites and facilities clean enough so no complaints.
Good location.freindly staff. Just a bit tired but looks like it will smarten up soon.

Very old tied park. Amenities dirty with spider webs  and broken tiles. Caravan site was difficult to navigate. Rained overnight leaving us with a van full of ants, which our neighbours expenced as well.

Absolutely digusting!  We stayed 14 nights and the toilets were only giving a quick clean on day 13. We spoke to another couple on day 3 of our stay who had just completed 14 nights and they said the toilets had been cleaned twice during their stay. I took photos everyday of the toilets as evidence.

We camped here for 2 nights in our caravan.
You basically pay for the location.
Nice spot close to go for a swim.
Lovely ride or walk along the coast. We did a ride with our kids aged 7,5 and 3. Some tough spots but it is worth it.
The amenities were okay for us.
10 minutes from city centre.
30 minutes from the gap and natural bridge.
There's a nice spot to swim near Middletown beach with shark nets and a pontoon. 15 minute ride with our kids on their bikes.
Lovely peaceful park right on the ocean helpful staff
Out of town away from hustle and bustle. Very good park with friendly welcoming and knowledgeable staff

I travelled the country try for 12 months staying in many caravan parks and this is by far the worst I have ever stayed in. The location of the park was nice but the “grass” site I was given was just a sand pit and the toilets and showers are absolutely disgusting. I do not think they actually get cleaned. I don’t have photos of the showers but they looked much the same as these urinals.

The location is perfect, this is probably the best view you will get in a cabin at a caravan Park in Albany.
Apparently RAC is taking over this park, it looks like the current management have stopped caring about the upkeep of the park. The facilities are run down the rooms are dirty (don't look under the bed or move any furniture for lost items it's disgusting)
If you are doing a multi day stay bring some cleaning basics as nothing is provided.
The price point is good and if RAC do take over this park it will make it more expensive and make holidaying in Emu poin out of reach for so many families.

Very disappointing experience.
The manager/owner was extremely rude, unprofessional, and clearly lacks basic manners and respect toward guests. Customer service was well below an acceptable standard.
Toilets and bathrooms were absolutely disgusting and poorly maintained.

I arrived around 7pm and called the owner to ask for an extension lead to plug into my van. Instead of a simple explanation, I was shouted at and told “I’m at home now, it’s your fault, don’t call anymore tonight.”
The owner lives right next to the caravan park, yet showed no courtesy or basic customer service.
Toilets and bathrooms were absolutely disgusting .
The only positive point is the good location. Unfortunately, the attitude and hygiene completely ruin the experience.

Wir raten von einem Besuch dieses Caravan Parks ab, wenn man sich erholen möchte! Es ist ein alter, heruntergekommener Platz, auf welchem auch so einiges kaputt ist. Am schlimmsten ist jedoch die nicht vorhandene Sauberkeit! Die Toiletten/Duschen sind massiv schmutzig, so dass man sie gar nicht benutzen möchte! Wir haben 4 Nächte gebucht, sind aber nach der 2. Nacht abgereist, da es für uns nicht akzeptabel war!

I do advise you not to stay here the whole site should be condemned to say the least. We were told on arrival that the wifi was broken and not fixed due to the owners selling the site off and not bothering to fix it. Not a great start to the day. The day got worse as our pitch was next door to an abandoned chalet in ruins been there for years abandoned. Next to the toilets building which was disgusting, terrible and a health hazard. Soiled sanitary wear lay in the showers with an abundance of old hair from previous residents, plus soiled toilet tissues lay abandoned on the floors, scrum like substance on all the doors, walls, floor. Totally disgusting and had not been cleaned for weeks. To top it off a old bra left on the wash basin full of grime. The rubbish bins were overflowing not been collected for days . The whole site shabby, derelict old shacks are everwhere, the owners don’t care as they have sold the site and are happy to take your money for their old retirement. Don’t stay here.

Avoid at all costs - disgusting, neglected and completely unacceptable

This was hands down one of the worst caravan park experiences we’ve ever had.

The park was infested with ants - absolutely everywhere. They were all through the site and inside the caravan, crawling through our belongings. It was relentless and revolting. Many people had been forced to place ant treatment down around their tents and caravans.

The amenities were beyond filthy. I genuinely don’t know how they’re still open. We’ve seen cleaner toilets in far more challenging parts of the world. There was no soap, the toilets were dirty, poorly maintained, and clearly not cleaned properly. The photos I've shared (taken at 8pm this evening) speak for themselves - judge from those.

The camp kitchen was disgusting. Dirty surfaces, unhygienic conditions, and not somewhere you’d want to prepare food. Thank God we were self-sufficient and didn’t need to use it.

Management? Security? Nowhere to be seen. They clearly did not care. No presence, no accountability, no effort. The out-of-hours number was out of order, so when things went wrong, there was no help.

The dump point was a health and safety nightmare — unhygienic, poorly maintained, and frankly shocking. It’s hard to believe this park is permitted to operate at all in its current state. Where is the compliance?!

The entire park feels neglected, tired, and completely mismanaged.

I’m posting this as a warning - particularly because I noticed a lot of young families with children staying here. Based on our experience, this is absolutely not somewhere I’d bring kids.

We stayed here over the holidays, when the park was full. We’d estimate well over 100-150 sites, all paying around $40-60 per night (give or take) - which is what we paid. Do the maths. That’s a huge amount of money coming in for a single night, yet the condition of the park was appalling. There is absolutely no excuse for amenities this dirty, kitchens this neglected, or basic supplies like soap being unavailable. With that level of income, this park could and should be maintained to a far higher standard, but clearly it isn’t —-and hasn’t been for some time.

We spent as little time as possible in the park because it was so unpleasant. Save yourself the stress and look elsewhere. This place needs serious intervention before it should be considered acceptable accommodation.

How do I explain it ….. for the first few days I was thinking, do the people who run or own this place care ! And I found out probably why they don’t , it’s because it’s getting knocked over and becoming an RAC Park allegedly.But explains it . Reason I say don’t care - the amenities were filthy , no deep cleans . There was built up urine/calcium under the unrinal  lips , from rolled toilet paper & stains through the back of the toilet lid hinges . The walls / tap ware were stained and they were cleaned once a day for 5 minutes. The Caravan sites were dusty & no rush to water vacant sites  to try repair or keep the dust down . Kids park , the sand was full of vegetation and rubbish wheelie bins were always overflowing. Only good thing is location.It is  the most I’ve used our vans toilet and shower . Felt dirty after using the amenities.|Couldn’t pay me to stay there again .

Absolutely filthy. This is without doubt the worst caravan park I’ve ever stayed in. Faeces on the walls of the toilets, no soap at the dump point (for four days) and management that didn’t care. Don’t stay here. A beautiful location with people managing it that couldn’t care less. Did I mention that there are ants everywhere?

Awsome spot close to beach but toilets and showers are dirty. No cleaner. There is a bucket and mop so you can clean the shower/toilet floor your self and a toilet brush so u can clean the toilet. You don’t pay to clean the amenities your self.
Great park out of town. Great staff, amenities dated but work fine. Dump point. Playground for kids. Level sites and some shady. Short walk to beaches and 10 minute drive to town.

Nice location if you manage to snag any good weather. Standard "old school" style, much of a muchness to any other 50 year old park really. Don't expect nice aesthetics, it's an old place without much maintenance done at all but cheaper than the alternatives.

People come for the beach location, not the park I would dare say. Extremely busy in peak periods, nice and quiet any other. Unfortunately, travelling 6mos on the road so far I can say the majority of caravan parks are pretty run down and stick to the bare minimum maintenance schedules, only doing what they absolutely have to. Have stopped expecting anything decent at this point until we get back home

The cleaners were nice people though and we didn't experience any problems per-se. If you're not at all fussy then should be ok here

Very nice place to stay very quiet and peaceful very friendly staff massive park and good facilities if you come to Albany Western Australia i would highly recommend to pop in and stay hear you will not be disappointed and very good prices thank you for the opportunity to have us stay
Very old run down caravan park. The big 4 parks in the area are much better

A great spot to stay in Albany at Emu point, pet friendly, good facilities and close to the ocean. And reasonably priced considering other option in the area. Unfortunately RAC has now purchased the property and I believe they will start renovations early next year.. Another park that will join the others $$$$$. Such a pity.
The local CAFE is only 10 minutes walking distance from the park, Great coffee and food choice is excellent. And it’s open from 7.30 for Breakfast, and closes at 4 pm. Facilities/tables along verandah for your fur child ..

The location of the park is excellent and if you are lucky enough to get the newest chalet which was clean, modern and had ocean views you are lucky. The staff seemed friendly on arrival. Unfortunately my husband and I stayed in the standard 2 bedroom villa the end of Oct 25. The villa was really old, smelled of mold which was heavily masked with an airfreshner smell. The net curtains were filthy, the fridge was rusty and the cooker and the floors & walls were dirty. The positives were there was plenty of hot water to get showered. I realise the park is very old  and discovered that it is going through a take over by RAC. I feel that the price charged for our accommodation was exceptionally high for the standard of accommodation we received.

We were booked into what we thought was a drive through site… I’ll freely admit I may have got that wrong but our site that we had was very very poor
3 sites… ourselves included all next to each other…. Rest of area empty

Site very uneven with a slope off the back…full of holes from rabbits … very easy to trip and fall in low light

Can’t for the life of me understand why when the area was empty they squeezed us into the worst three spots

We stayed three nights in a powered site with our rooftop tent and felt a sense of peace and relaxation like nowhere else during our trip. The atmosphere is calm and welcoming, perfect for resting after days on the road.
Everything was great — the bathrooms are modern and spotless, and the whole area is very well maintained. The camp kitchen is a bit old, but it was regularly cleaned and had everything we needed.
Overall, a beautiful and peaceful stay. We strongly recommend this place to anyone visiting Albany!
Great location right next to the beach and Dog friendly

Reminded me of a trailer park in the US you see on the movies!
Lots of permanents, older males in poor living conditions must be so cold for the poor buggers.
Cabins are old and along with the caravan sites are very overpriced. Would not return nor recommend.

Staff eating and location 5 starts.
Bays for caravans 1 star. Not level, blocked by a wall while all flat concrete bays were free with easy access. Not sure why I was put on grass in a difficult position.
Facilities 1 star. Old and worn out. Labels on washing machine and dryer rubbed off. Dryer does not dry. Bathrooms constantly wet and shower curtains need to be larger so they cover the opening properly.
But did I mention the staff!!! Second to none. They were fantastic and helpful and friendly.

Currently staying here, tonight will be night 4/4. Washed our white clothes in their washing machine for $4 and they came out absolutely filthy. We advised reception who said “we’ll send someone to have a look at it”. No apology and no offer to return the $4 or pay to rewash the potentially ruined clothing. Will go to the laundry mat in town at our expense. This is not good enough. Only reason the room wasn’t 1 star is because we’re on a site and don’t know about the rooms. Also not “beachside” cannot even access the beach here. I can see why they’re 3.1 out of 5. I should’ve checked the reviews.

Great park. Terrific position. Facilities were spotless. Laundry is a bit dated but there are other more modern ones in town. Only real downside in the park is that travellers are always data-hungry and there is no wifi. We will definitely stay again.

Amazing location with budget accommodation options and some really nice units on the beach front. You can fish from the rocks right at the front of the park. Lots of sites of all different shapes and sizes. Nice park and playground right next door and you can walk right on to Middleton Beach from the western side of the property. Walk around emu point to the cafe and protected waters and boat ramp.

Paid for premium beach front site|We get a grass plot with deep holes|No shade and a row of “chalets” in front and a road then rock wall. |No beach, you have to walk about 100 mtrs to a small causeway. |The bathroom facilities are newish, bright and clean. Probably it’s only saving grace. |That’s the only reason it’s got 2 stars

Nice quiet old park full of permanents.

Can't read the site markings on the road as they're so old, so finding your's is problematic.

The electricity connection points aren't easy or obvious to find, and ours was hidden behind trees. Ask the staff to help find it, and you get this patronising attitude why you can't find something poorly marked and hidden behind a bushy tree!

They have amenities suitable for those with mobility problems, but you can't use them as they're locked!

Water and sullage points are also poorly located and not close to sites.

It's an overpriced run-down dump!
We had a two bedroom cabin. It had great  views, but it was a little dated and needs a spruce up. It had bbq facilities, but check out the photo
to old
Soap dispenser poor quality and road need attention

Is everything we want or need in for our caravan holiday, plenty of space, fantastic beach front location and wonderful staff all at a great price. Thankyou for yet another lovely stay, see you again soon

Sites have some grass and concrete slabs. Unfortunately ours has a crack and I tripped and face planted. The sympathy from the office was underwhelming. |The recreation room was disgustingly dirty and neglected. |Shower blocks were clean and  modern and showers hot.|Location is excellent.

The location is lovely, at Emu Point, even though it is a little out of the way of town.
The park itself is large but we felt there weren't enough ablution blocks and the one nearest to us, but still far away, was rather old. The soap, for washing your hands, was so watered down, it ran straight through my hands. At night there weren't any lights on the path, so you had to use a torch to light the way to the ablutions. The ground is very uneven, potholes and speed bumps, so make sure to take a torch. Also, once inside the ablutions there were no lights, which is a little unnerving when you are on your own.
The tent area was located next to the campers kitchen and dump point (to empty caravan/RV toilets). Also, the tents were crammed together. I'm not sure if it was a large family or strangers but it looked very cramped.
The caravan site we had, number 5, was a decent size, once we got our van in place but it took a little manoeuvring to get in and out and would have been more difficult if our lovely neighbours didn't move their cars for us. We had a concrete pad, which was ideal and we had room to park our car, however, the person opposite us parked his car outside the fence, as he didn't have enough room to park near his caravan.
If you book here, make sure to take some ant killer, as everybody around us, including us, had ants everywhere. We spent a lot of time killing them around our site and in our caravan.
The park has a rule that by 10pm there is to be no noise. Thankfully, everybody seemed to adhere to this rule. Everybody seemed to be courteous and friendly, as people generally seem to be in caravan parks, as we all want a good experience.
Overall I would recommend this caravan park, however, as mentioned earlier, take a torch, ant killer and be prepared to be patient when parking your caravan.

Thank you for a lovely stay over the Easter Break.
First time staying and a great little spot near the water.
Wonderful service and we loved the spot near the office. Park was full and the kids enjoyed making new friends and playing on the kids playground.

Sehr unsauberes Zimmer. Alles total veraltet  Rost am ganzen Dach und auf der Veranda.
Stinkt nach Urin im Badezimmer, da unten ein offener Abfluss ist. Aus dem Abfluss
sprießen  Blumen raus. Man hat einfach die klobürste über den Abfluss gestellt damit es keiner sehen kann.. Als wir die Rezeption angesprochen haben hat man uns einfach gesagt "es ist halt so wie es ist" keine vergünstigungen aufgrund der Mängel und total unfreundlich. Das Preisleistungsverhältnis ist absolut schlecht für das Zimmer was man bekommt. Das einzig positive war, daß man direkt am Ozean war...

My husband tells me not to say “I’m never” going to do something again. Well I can say I’m never going back to this caravan park again. We returned to our caravan about 10.30pm to find approximately 1,000 ants crawling around inside our caravan and probably more than that on the outside. My husband sprayed inside and outside of the caravan then I spent the next 2 hours washing walls and stripping beds, finally falling into bed around 1am. The next day wasn’t spent sightseeing, we had washing to do, more ants to kill, washing the outside of the caravan. An ANT INFESTATION.  IF WE HAD BEEN TOLD ABOUT THE ANT PROBLEM WHEN BOOKING IN we could have put ant powder around. We weren’t the only ones with the ant problem, there were many other caravans and tents with the ant problem. Regrettably Management knew about this problem months ago.

Photos don't match the park in gday app, felt like false advertising. It says a lot that the first rule they have is once your checked in you can't get a refund. We left after one night after paying for 4. If you have kids go to the big4 next door.

The park response below to Tiah is nothing short of passing the buck. It absolutely should not be up to your guests to inform them that hot water gas bottles are empty. Put some monitors on them and stop blaming others for your incompetence

We placed our caravan booking well in advance two months, and what site did we get, a bloody sandpit, no grass or concrete slab, park has no street lights, left in the dark, not a very good holiday, will not be back here ever.
Older style caravan park, but good value and clean ablutions in a great location.

During my 4 night stay here, the “new” bathrooms didn’t have any hot water. Upon communicating this with management, I was told “You need to let the water run for quite a while before it gets hot.  We know it wastes water but that is how they work”. For context, I ran the hot water tap for 3 minutes at least!
The camp kitchen facilities were incredibly faulty the whole time, with myself and other campers all struggling to either get the gas on or stay lit.
The response from management was very passive aggressive, felt like they alluded to me being an idiot and was just overall rude.

Really lovey park with nice atmosphere,relaxing and right on the beach,toilets.could do with regular cleaning as nothing was done the whole weekend we were there,even emptied the mop bucket which stank,need to have pinoclean in it or something,hand wash was pritty much watered down dish liquid....I'd still stay again as it was a nice park

We stayed there for a week...when we got there we were greeted with mold in the bathroom, hair in the drawers, the toilet was dirty, a hole behind the toilet pipes, the plug has mold in it, the PVC piping on the outside wasnt even attached, dust on the bedhead, the kitchen sink plug was absolutely discusting, the floor needed sweeping, the door locks hardly worked, stained couch and one of the chairs legs are bent, marks on the walls which i dont know what they were, the outside needed a sweep, cobwebs everywhere inside and out....We paid alot of money to stay there...We won't be staying there again...

Location, location, location. I really enjoyed our stay at Rose Garden for the most part. I agree with the other reviews, the park is in dyer need of some maintenance and the loos could be cleaned more often. However,  I think most people will agree that 5 star camping comes at a much higher price! The Big 4 next door was $130p/n for a powered site!!!! Rose Garden was a mere $54p/n.
Also, in Rose Gardens defence...I have never seen so many unsupervised, unruly and destructive children in all my life! They were the culprits behind much of the filth in the amenity blocks and the vandalism of the recreation room. The park management can't be expected to supervise and continually clean up ablutions after these little ferals, whilst their lazy parents sit back with a beer in their hands.

I had the pleasure of staying at Acclaim Rose Gardens Caravan Park in Emu Point, and it truly exceeded all my expectations. The park’s location is unbeatable, just a stone’s throw from the beautiful Emu Point Beach, which is perfect for families. The area is very kid-friendly, with calm, shallow waters ideal for young children to swim and play safely. It’s the kind of beach where you can relax knowing your little ones are having a great time in a safe environment.

The park itself is a wonderful place for families, with excellent amenities that make it easy to settle in and enjoy your stay. The facilities are clean, modern, and well-maintained, catering to both adults and children. The staff are incredibly friendly and helpful. Their warmth and attentiveness really stand out and make you feel right at home. The park itself is shaded and spacious and the sites are generous in size.

Acclaim Rose Gardens is also a fantastic base for exploring Albany and the surrounding areas. Whether you’re visiting nearby beaches, the local wildlife, or exploring Albany’s historic sites, you’ll find plenty to do within easy reach.

I highly recommend Acclaim Rose Gardens Caravan Park to families looking for a relaxing, safe, and convenient place to stay in Albany. It’s the perfect spot to enjoy the great outdoors, spend quality time with loved ones, and explore the beautiful region.

Just stayed at a powered site for the fourth year straight and was very clear how run down it is. The location is awesome, and it’s ’ok’ to stay here, but could be so much better if they watered the grass, had an no smoking policy that was enforced (a guest was smoking during a conversation with a staff member - manager maybe), increased cleaning (time? Skill? Effort?) and  improved facilities (games room needs attention, kids playground is tired and dated, movie night or free pancake morning/sausage sizzle dinner could work well, and there are multiple old buildings boarded up/decommissioned). Rose gardens needs a reason for people to come there - something to hang its hat on, as the location or cost isn’t enough any more.  I wouldn’t say I won’t stay here again, but am looking at the other parks, as are our family and friends.

If I could give negative stars, this campsite would earn every single one. It was an absolute nightmare from start to finish, and I wouldn’t wish the experience on my worst enemy.

To start, the campsite was overrun with ants. Not a few ants here and there—an infestation that quickly invaded our tent and ruined any chance of comfort. Our clothes and sleeping bag was crawling with them, and no amount of cleaning or ant spray could keep them at bay.

Then there were the toilets. I’ve seen cleaner facilities in abandoned buildings. These were hands-down the most disgusting toilets I’ve ever had the misfortune of encountering. I’ve seen better toilets in some 3rd world countries. Overflowing, foul-smelling, and seemingly untouched by any kind of maintenance/cleaning staff. It was enough to make you sick just stepping near them.

The shower facilities weren’t any better. They were grimy and mould-ridden. Calling them “facilities” is generous. They looked more like a health hazard than a place to wash.

As if that weren’t enough, the park itself was completely insecure there are no cameras and no lighting in key areas. “Fencing” that anyone could simply walk past. As a result, we were robbed during our stay. Our bag  was stolen during the night leaving us feeling unsafe and violated. We reported it, but there was no help or even concern from the management—just a shrug and a “that happens sometimes” attitude.

This campsite is a disgrace. If you value your health, safety, or sanity, avoid it at all costs.

Our stay at Rose gardens was an absolute nightmare. From the moment we arrived, it was clear that this site was poorly maintained and far from welcoming. Here’s why I strongly recommend avoiding this park:
1.	ANTS EVERYWHERE! The grounds were teeming with ants. They invaded our caravan, made their way into our food, and were impossible to get rid of. It was a constant battle and completely ruined our stay.
2.	Gross Facilities: The amenities were filthy and clearly not cleaned regularly enough for the number of campers. There were two toilet blocks; the first which was fairly modern, had code entry to access yet seems to have been vandalised with paint stripper. The other one was not locked, I assume this is because no homeless person would ever think of using such a disgusting shower block. No soap, blocked toilet every day, absolutely filthy. The laundry area was no better,  once I had found it amount the multiple derelict buildings that exist on the site. During our stay we were told not to use the dump point as it was blocked, but we’re not given any advice as to an alternative.

3.	Poor Security: There was absolutely no sense of safety here. No gates, no fence and no cameras. Unfortunately, during our stay, a bag was stolen from our site, and the park management advised us to contact the police, not their fault I know but I am really surprised that G’day parks don’t at least record incidents when they happen. This lack of security following the theft made us feel incredibly vulnerable.

This was, without a doubt, the worst caravan site we have ever stayed at. The location is fantastic and with some new investment and some TLC could be a great park. But until that happens save yourself the trouble and book elsewhere. At the moment this place doesn’t even deserve one star.

If i could give a zero star review  i would.
Dirty bathroom and laundry facilities 🤢
huge ant infestation throughout park affecting multiple tents and caravans (including ours),  unkempt games room,  blocked dump point, multiple thefts. across park, park owner / manager COULD NOT care less!
Shame on you G'day /acclaim parks for putting your name to this place.
Definitely would NOT recommend or will ever go back 😡

Avoid This Park at All Costs – The Least Relaxing and Most Stressful Stay

If you’re looking for a holiday, do not stay here. This was genuinely the least relaxing and most stressful experience we’ve ever had at a holiday park.

Ant Infestation
From the moment we arrived, the site was completely infested with ants. Every tree and slab was riddled with them. The small site we were assigned was covered in ant powder, which is toxic to dogs – a major concern as we were traveling with one. By Day 2, ants were everywhere inside our van, including the kids’ bunk beds and walls, despite us not leaving any food out. Our neighbors were also dealing with the same issue and informed us that previous occupants had complained, leaving ant powder everywhere.

When we reported this to the office, staff claimed they were unaware of the problem – a laughable statement given the infestation across the park. The staff’s solution was dismissive at best. When we requested ant spray, we were met with attitude and told they couldn’t spray because of our dog. While they begrudgingly gave us one can, it was insufficient, forcing us to purchase additional bait and spray ourselves. Despite our efforts, every morning we woke to ants throughout the van.

Lack of Maintenance and Cleanliness
The site appeared neglected, with no evidence of maintenance between visitors. Bathrooms were filthy even after being “cleaned,” with a single hand sanitizer dispenser for the block – often empty. The dump point was out of action, and instead of offering a solution or apology, the park sent out a curt message: “Dump point is out of action, please stop messaging us.”

Security Issues
The park has zero security. While guests are given swipe cards for vehicle access, an open drive with a chain down allows anyone to enter freely. There’s no fencing, CCTV, or adequate lighting to deter trespassers. During our stay, our family was robbed, and when reported to the office, staff showed no concern or willingness to assist, stating we needed to file the police report ourselves. They also admitted to being approached by other guests who had been robbed. We never felt safe or secure during our stay.

Poor Facilities and Dangerous Hazards
The activity room was filthy, with no balls for games and mold in the washing machines. A protruding screw on a door injured our daughter, and when we reported it to the front desk, no action was taken to fix the hazard. It wasn’t until we mistakenly emailed the wrong park that someone followed up on the issue for us.

Final Thoughts
The only positive aspect of this park is its proximity to Emu Point. However, when compared to the well-maintained neighboring chalets and lawns, it’s clear the filthy, ant-infested conditions here are due to neglect, not location.

This park is managed by staff who deny problems and blame their customers, ensuring nothing will ever improve. We chose this park because of its affiliation with G’Day Parks, expecting a trusted experience, but we were sorely mistaken.

We’ve been camping for 10 years across South and Western Australia, and this was by far the worst experience we’ve ever had. Save yourself the stress and choose a different park.

Shame on you G’Day/Acclaim parks, If I could give this campsite 0 stars I would, from the first day of our week long stay, the manager/owner at the reception was rude to my family on multiple occasions, complete lack of care on their behalf. The second day here our caravan was completely covered in ants, inside the van and out, nothing like we had ever seen before and after telling the reception they did nothing about it, instead they told us they didn’t know there was an ant problem which was a complete lie by the evidence of ant powder all over the sites and multiple campers near us making us aware of the situation.

The toilet block was a complete an utter joke, they claim they “clean everyday even on public holidays!” But by the evidence of dead roaches, empty soap dispensers and rusted nail sticking out of the door at a height which could hit someone’s eye, it does not seem that there cleaner is there for anymore than 2 minutes a day.

Dump point was blocked for a few days, definitely not ideal in a completely packed campsite during the holiday season.

Finally, we got robbed! Stolen was a UHF radio and a hiking bag, informed reception and they had absolutely no care, stating that campers had been letting them know all day of items that had been stolen, they did not bother to make a note or report to the police (assuming they wouldn’t as it would give the campsite a bad look) so we had to file a report to the police ourselves. No CCTV cameras either of course!

Do yourself a favour and don’t stay here, this campsite made what should of been a fun holiday in Albany, stressful and uncomfortable.

Website photos are highly misleading!! No green grass, b/r at lower end are old & dirty - cleaning during peak holidays ('24/'25) only 1xp/d. Dumping point has overflowed with campers to be relocated; water pressure built up at campers' kitchen and urinal overflown in men's b/r. Great for dog owners, but disgusting facilities!!

Ants everywhere. I saw a beach towel stuffed into a toilet bowl that remained for two days. This caravan park is lawless. Games room hasn’t been cleaned in months - spider webs, broken furniture, could have shoveled the dirt from the floor - real crack house vibes.

Reception people friendly great location.
Facilities insufficient for the amount of guests and not clean, I checked them all out all the same, haven't been cleaned properly in a while.

We did not stay in the holiday units. We stayed in the caravan park behind. THE CARAVAN PARK IS AWFUL. We have been here every year for the last 4 years. The place is poorly kept and run down. The facilities are dirty and unclean. Again we came this year at xmas to give it another chance, but nothing changes. Still dirty. It is peak period and only 3 of the 5 showers are working in the main amenities block. The same clumps of hair and soap stay for days in the showers. The lawns are not watered or cared for so it is always sandy with dying grass. No effort or expense is used to make the place reach even a low standard. It is a disgrace.
Excellent Caravan Park right on the water. Lots of beautiful birds. The roses are beautiful too. I'll definitely come back to this Park. ❤️

Can’t beat the ocean front location and Jolene at reception was soo incredibly helpful assisting over the phone and as I was checking in with an elderly person, contacted me to advise of steps. Great experience and wonderful view to wake up to.
Nice spot, close to Kalgan River cruise, which we did. Beach access & walking paths.

The general facilities looked like something people first see when they check into a Summer School Camp. There were moths outside everywhere at dusk all around the camp kitchen, there was dust all over the floor in the recreation room, the bathrooms were smellier than usual with limited space in the showers and the gate entrance was broken down. Overall, this place was OK to a certain extent.
Attacked by resident Magpies. Don't go in breeding season

Nice place to stay great location.
But when you have to put $18 into a drier to dry clothes and there still wet not so happy

Appalling chalets. Mould in shower. Beds not made up when I arrived. Beds smelt of perspiration strongly.   Curtains not fitting window therefore no privacy. Owner/management very rude and condescending. When I mentioned how bad the mould was his reply was “depends on how much mould”.  Insisted I booked on line, when infact I phone to book.  Refused upgrade or to refund my $740 I had paid for 4 nights in this disgusting place
Over due for a make over
Nice cabins on the foreshore

Nice park to stay but a little dated. Reception staff need to make you feel a little more welcome as definitely no warmth felt on arrival or in any interactions. Amenities were very clean and above standard. Rest of park seemed old and dated. Needs to be 2 dump points as long way to walk if over the far side of park given size. Large spaces so did not feel cramped in but road level to sites in some places needs lowering given we observed some caravans had difficulty unhitching. Easy access to walkways and beach and local cafe. Permanents need to adhere to same speed limit as guests as our observations was that some use roads as race track. I almost got hit as one resident speed with a trailer in haste to get to wherever he was going. Lots of little bunnies, which was good to see wildlife and reasonably quiet. Overall good place to stay. Dog friendly which is a plus.

We had a powered site . I rang the day before to see if they had 4 powered sites available as we were travelling with friends, when we arrived we were met by the most rudest woman ever, her name was Barbara , I explained we had rang the day before and they had said no need to book as there were plenty of sites available , then she huffed and said it would take her 10 minutes each to book us in and we said that was fine we had plenty of time , her attitude was disgusting and should not be working in a industry where you greet people , we won’t be back and lots of friends won’t be either .

We had a caravan site for 3 nights recently .
Could not have been more pleasantly surprised !
Perfect location , quiet , well sized sites , spotlessly clean amenities.
Easy book in - staff were friendly and efficient- and the resident rabbits added to the quirkiness
Would not hesitate to recommend!
Julie , with Robert Coker
Had a nice stay .

During our stay over Mother's Day, we encountered some serious issues that really put a damper on things. From black mold to a leaky toilet flooding the bathroom floor, broken front doors, and a shower curtain that was hung so high it practically turned the bathroom into a swimming pool after every shower, it was a mess. And to top it all off, my partner woke up with ringworm on day 3, to which we were told "oh well. we have no other complaints about it". The cherry on top was reading through the management's rude responses to negative reviews on Google. Instead of owning up to the problems, they get defensive and nasty. It's a shame that they'd rather argue than admit this place, or at least the chalet we stayed in, needs serious cleaning and fixing.

edit: and if you would like to do research that's ringworm of the SCALP. ringworm on the body can show up much, much earlier but thanks again for your lack of accountability 👍

I stayed in the park last year for few weeks.
Their bed has fleas everywhere. When I first complained, the receptionist question me where is the flea coming from and refuse to change room. My legs got so bad. They only offered to change the bed sheet.  I ring the place again. I believe the owner answered the phone this time and investigate on the bed. They admitted that they have fleas on the bed and eventually changed the room. However, half a year later I still have all the scaring from the fleas bite last year. I am super upset regard the scaring. So I just want to let every know that this happen and try to not book in to this place.
Just love this area. Right on the water great walks.
Great position right on water

Check in and left out of hours.  No issues with, everything went smoothly.

Great location and easy to walk around Bay Area.  Amenities older & smaller in some parts but clean and tidy.

My powered site for van was large and backed onto the tennis courts (some early morning noise), camp kitchen large but possibly very busy in peak times.

Camp kitchen next to tent area and can be quite noisy.

Dog friendly, would stay again
Very friendly and hard working reception, nice shower facilities, clean kitchen cleaned twice daily, very good location and a quiet site. What else can one ask for? Highly recommended!
Nice quiet park. Location is terrific.  Clean amenities,  very accommodating staff.  Most sites are easily accessible
Staff are friendly, great location, well looked after park , very popular c/van park with us travelling oldies

Awful! Don’t stay here.
Location is the only good thing about this park.
Our designated site when we arrived,did not fit our van even though you give them the size on booking. We have been here 2 weeks and witnessed the same thing on multiple occasions.
Toilets are disgusting and always out of toilet paper and hand wash. No lighting at night time. Place is full of rabbit holes which is a safety issue if you walk anywhere in the park after sunset.
Dry & very dusty. Lawns are dead and some trees have very large dead branches, which according to permanent residents, often fall, so beware and stay at your own risk.
We both love Rose Garden caravan park,close to emu point and restaurant for brekkie & lunch

Stayed here for 4 night….
Great location, some of the park nice the site we were on was horrible.
Bins not emptied for the 4 night still full when we left….is some really nice looking newish ammenities, filthy when we arrived cleaned 3 days later…. Would not blame management for this they would be on a budget from Acclaim head office….
And at $50 per night, I know who is taking the cream off the top.
2 nd Acclaim we have stayed at and never again if I can help it.
Cheers

We absolutely loved our time here. We came for 5 days and stayed for 10 days. The park backs right on to the bay at Emu Point and Middleton Beach. You can have your dogs off lead at Middleton Beach but not allowed at Emu Point. It's only about 8km to Albany for all your shopping and dining requirements.

The park is well maintained, but must mention that there are a lot of small holes all around the grounds from rabbits. Unfortunately, the Council is very slack in tending to the problem in the entire area.

Apart from that, the park is pet friendly, has a play area for kids, the camp kitchen and amenities were very clean and the sites are pretty spacious. Some sites have slabs whilst others have a bit of grass or sand but all looked reasonably level.

It's right next door to the Big 4 but a lot cheaper as it doesn't have all the pools and slides etc for the kids, but as grey nomads, we don't need that nor the noise.

We would certainly recommend this park, which has very friendly and obliging staff, if you're looking for a quiet,  relaxing stay.

What can I say? The Rose Garden caravan park is in need of some urgent attention.We have stayed many times but this will be our last. Yes it was school holidays but the ablution blocks were dirty,poorly maintained,mould in the showers but watered down hand soap was supplied. Generally the place is old and run down and what is newer is in need of more regular cleaning and maintenance .
Awsome place to stay. Clean facilities close to the beach and local cafe... Just Brilliant!

This was a lovely location 👌
The washing machines and dryers could do with an upgrade it costs a fortune to do a wash n dry if it's raining otherwise put on the line..
The toilet seat kept coming off and the main bedroom fan sounded like a helicopter taking off! haha
The other upgrades would have to be,
Linen
Lounge and frypans..
Other than that everything else was perfect.

Not wingeing just suggesting a few upgrades.

There are places much nicer that charge less per night..

But I chose to stay here for the peace and the view..
Lots of rabbits running around if you love rabbits haha.
The owners were lovely and helpfull to talk to and the grounds looked clean and tidy .
Beach view and walk across the road for an early morning swim
Great little family park.
Would definitely stay here again.

We were a little disappointed by our stay. On day 2 kids rode bikes through our unpowered tent site at speed and ripped the fly on our tent. They didn't even stop and no parents in sight. Also our food in the fridge was thrown out even though we abided by the rules. The sites are pretty sandy too. That said, the toilets, showers and camp kitchen were clean and nice. The location is great.

Stayed here over new years, arrived as 3 families with 3 sites. Were positioned along the road side where there is no fence or any kind of security. Site sizes were tiny. Toilets are so bad that when checking in we were told to net even use them. Laminated sign in reception about "skid marks" on the toilet bowls.

Definitely will never be there again and would not recommend to anyone. Steer well clear folks.
Lovely caravan park with clean amenities and in a lovely area

Clean, tidy and everything is functioning. Nice, flat and grassy caravan sites.
Good playground and a spacious recreation room.
Good camp kitchen.
Lots to see and do in and around the Caravan Park.

This was our third visit to this park, and I'm still full of mixed feelings.

We've been to a lot of places way better than this. But we have also seen a lot that were way worse.

We stayed in a tent on the edge of the park, opposite the camp kitchen. The biggest issue was the noise coming from the building next to the park, which sounded like a commercial refrigerator. When we complained about this to management they said that they can't do anything about the noise, but allowed us to select a better spot.

Please be aware that there are not many tent spots in this park! And the one that we had looked like the best one. Others were not very level, or in the backyards of the cottages, or sloped and windy. The management didn't allow us to use a caravan site for our tent.

It looks like the park was not being looked after. The amenities weren't very clean, the tv remote in the game room was missing, and the room was never locked for the night time. Some buildings would greatly benefit from some TLC.

On the flip side, the location is great! There's a nice cafe couple of hundred metres down the road, a bike path, nice views, rabbits, etc. Not too far from the town.

Had a good booking experience with female staff who waived the 5 night minimum for me to 3 nights. Stayed in a small mostly sandy powered tent site right next to the camp kitchen. The flood lights remained on all night causing the tents to be brightly lit inside all night. The next day on enquiring if the lights could be turned off at night the male manager rudely informed me it was government regulation they stay on and part of his duty of care and terminated the call without offering any solution or site change. I had to park between the kitchen and my tents to block some light the next night and it was still bright. Never returning and will not recommend this place. Only redeeming factor is the location and the abundance of rabbits which kept my son entertained. The playground and toilets are a good distance from the tent area. Toilets and table tennis table are in bad shape.

Nice older style park. Greeted by the Rose garden at the entrance.  Lots of old slabs, many sites not very level, some really tiny sites, others quite huge. Love them or hate them the rabbits do make a mess of the grounds. Watch your step.
Great caravan park located in a beautiful location. All amenities were clean and well maintained. Bit old fashioned but that's what I like. Well worth a try!
Great spot to base yourself for Albany area. Large grassy sites, great walking track alongside the beach. Beach allows dogs off leash. Would stay here again.

Absolutely worst place in Albany to stay. Wouldn’t suggest anyone to come this place as the staff is so rude and won’t even talk to you in manners. Ground is full of holes and yet they don’t take the responsibilities for it and they won’t budge at all. You have to pay upfront the full amount and yet no hospitality
Lovely park, quiet and close to town.
We camped here for a night. Great facilities, clean and nice and quiet. Excellent location.
